title: Chocolate tart
subtitle: Sweet shortcrust case with a dark chocolate ganache filling
excerpt: A sweet shortcrust pastry case, blind-baked and cooled, filled with a chocolate ganache of dark chocolate, double cream, and butter, then left to set at room temperature rather than in the fridge. Refrigerating the ganache makes it too firm and dulls the surface; a room-temperature set produces the correct glossy, yielding texture that cuts cleanly with a hot knife.
sourceNotes: The chocolate tart (tarte au chocolat) is a standard of French pâtisserie made with a pâte sucrée (sweet shortcrust) and a ganache filling. It reached British restaurant menus in the 1980s and became established as a home recipe through the 1990s and 2000s as dark chocolate became more widely available at higher cocoa percentages. The recipe here uses a ratio of two parts chocolate to one part cream for a filling that sets firm enough to cut but remains yielding rather than hard. A small amount of butter added to the warm ganache gives the finished surface a gloss.

content.text: The ganache filling must set at room temperature for the right texture and gloss. The

takes 2 to 3 hours. Do not try to speed it up with the fridge. The finished tart should hold its shape when cut but give under the fork. A hot, dry knife (run under hot water and wiped before each cut) gives the cleanest slices.

attrs.prepNote: yolk only, mixed with 2 tbsp cold water

content.text: Make the pastry: rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add the egg yolk and water and bring together into a smooth dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll out to 3 mm and line a 23 cm tart tin. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Blind-bake with baking paper and beans for 15 minutes, remove the paper and beans, and bake for a further 8 minutes until golden and dry. Leave to cool completely.

For the ganache, place the chopped chocolate in a bowl. Heat the cream until barely simmering, pour over the chocolate, and leave for 3 minutes without stirring. Stir from the centre outward until completely smooth. Add the softened butter and stir until it disappears and the ganache is glossy. Pour into the cooled pastry case, tilting to level. Leave to set at cool room temperature for 2 to 3 hours until firm enough to cut. Scatter with sea salt flakes before serving if desired.
